core_mechanism: Analysis of the Lyapunov-exponent spectrum of a high-dimensional iterated map to detect a crossover between two scaling regimes (long-range vs. short-range) tuned by a single correlation/localization length scale, contrasting correlated vs. uncorrelated randomness in the underlying linear operator
brief_reason: The abstract studies the Lyapunov spectrum scaling of iterated unitary maps and searches for the crossover from long-range to short-range scaling as the localization length ξ decreases, comparing correlated (quasiperiodic) against uncorrelated (Anderson) disorder.
